title: Parenting Skills Classes * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parenting Skills Classes * Domestic Violence Issues 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE HELPING CHILDREN EXPOSED TO BATTERERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting skills classes in order to teach parents to talk to and listen to their children about their experiences with domestic violence. Rather than a comprehensive parenting skills class, the focus is upon domestic violence and how it relates to parenting issues. Child care and transportation may be arranged. 103 S Kerby Avenue Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers who have experienced domestic violence Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 592-2515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE HELPING CHILDREN EXPOSED TO BATTERERS
